items.title: Bevrijdende zege helpt VVV weer richting plek voor play-offs
items.summary: VVV-Venlo heeft zaterdag in De Koel voor het eerst in dit kalenderjaar gewonnen. Tegen FC Eindhoven maakte Naïm Matoug het verschil, na een geweldige doelpoging van Bjorn van Zijl.

items.title: Swingend NEC ook met wat geluk naar tweede plek in Eredivisie
items.summary: NEC heeft voor in ieder geval driekwart dag de tweede plek in de Eredivisie in handen. Zaterdagavond werd Heracles Almelo verslagen met mooi voetbal, hoewel de ploeg uit Nijmegen ook wat geluk had dat Lequincio Zeefuik tot tweemaal toe op precies dezelfde plek van de linkerpaal mikte.

items.title: VI Live: Nuytinck maakt na tien maanden rentree bij feestend NEC
items.summary: Zaterdag staan er vier Eredivisie-wedstrijden op het programma, maar ook in het buitenland wordt gevoetbald. Onder meer Manchester United Arsenal, Barcelona, Borussia Dortmund en Napoli komen in actie. Volg al het voetbal in de VI Live.

items.title: Barça maakt geen fout dankzij imposante Lewandowski en doelpuntenmachine Lamine
items.summary: Barcelona heeft zaterdag een nieuwe stap gezet richting prolongatie van de Spaanse landstitel. De koploper van La Liga won in eigen huis relatief makkelijk van Mallorca en slaat zo een gat van vier punten met achtervolger Real Madrid, dat zondag weer dichterbij kan komen.

items.title: Mainoo embodies Man United's resurgence under Carrick in win vs. Spurs
items.summary: Kobbie Mainoo looked on his way out of Manchester United as they struggled under Ruben Amorim. Now he's pivotal in their revival under Michael Carrick.

items.title: Spot-on Cole Palmer fires first-half hat-trick as Chelsea win at woeful Wolves
items.summary: It would be easy to paint this as a Cole Palmer masterclass, the Chelsea midfielder completing his hat-trick inside the opening 38 minutes, but it is fair to say by the time he was substituted on the hour, his work was done. At that point it was 3-1 to the visitors, Wolves pulling a goal back early in the second half when Tolu Arokodare spun in the box to convert at a corner. From there, if not earlier, it was hard not to feel as though Chelsea were going through the motions. Palmer’s trio of cool finishes, two from the penalty spot, earned victory and his third, to cap a slick team move, was surely the most pleasing for Liam Rosenior. Rosenior’s record as Chelsea head coach now reads seven wins in nine matches and four successive victories in the Premier League. Continue reading...

items.title: Newcastle United v Brentford: Premier League – live
items.summary: ⚽ Premier League updates from the 5.30pm GMT kick-off ⚽ Live scores | Tables | Follow us on Bluesky | Mail Dominic It’s around about this time that I ask you for your score predictions – send them to me via email if you wish. I shall also remind you that the last three games I’ve live-blogged for the Guardian have brought four goals – two 3-1 home wins and a 2-2 draw. So I’m predicting a 3-1 Newcastle win to continue the trend. Wissa would just love a goal, wouldn’t he? And it’s about time Howe’s boys got back on track at home. Continue reading...

items.title: Palmer schrijft geschiedenis met wéér een vroege hattrick
items.summary: Cole Palmer heeft geschiedenis geschreven in de uitwedstrijd van Chelsea tegen Wolverhampton Wanderers. De Chelsea-ster scoorde voor rust al drie keer tegen de hekkensluiter van de Premier League. Aston Villa liet als concurrent van Chelsea punten liggen bij AFC Bournemouth.

items.title: Gyökeres double keeps Arsenal on title track in comfortable win over Sunderland
items.summary: There are times when the best thing for a side is an uneventful win. Titles are won less in the big set-piece games than against mid-table sides in the easily forgotten circumstances of a Saturday afternoon. Arsenal weren’t brilliant against Sunderland, but they were good enough to win comfortably, and that increases their lead at the top of the table to nine points, adding a degree of extra pressure to Manchester City’s Sunday visit to Liverpool. In a way, this was the platonic ideal of Mikel Arteta’s football. Not a huge amount happened but most of what did was in or around the Sunderland box. It was bitty, stop-start, built around set-plays and devoid of much in the way of imagination or spontaneity. Coaches obsessed by pressing patterns probably loved it, but it will not live long in the wider collective memory. For a long time the game seemed in danger of being mutually respected out of existence, both teams watching, probing warily, even if in the end Arsenal’s probing proved far more dangerous. Continue reading...

items.title: Pressure piles on Scott Parker as West Ham sink Burnley in basement battle
items.summary: For Burnley this felt like the beginning of their Premier League farewell tour, while West Ham reignited their fight for survival. In truth, there was little between the two but the Hammers showed why they have hope of staying up, providing the brief moments of quality, something the Lancastrians could not. Crysencio Summerville and Taty Castellanos expertly finished from two perfect assists to send West Ham to victory, leaving them three points adrift of Nottingham Forest in 17th. Burnley showed fight in the second half but a 16th straight game without a win, including 11 defeats, brought derision from the home supporters and demands for change in the dugout and boardroom. Continue reading...

items.title: Arsenal move nine points clear after hard-fought win over Sunderland
items.summary: Arsenal piled the pressure on title rivals Manchester City with a 3-0 victory over Sunderland at the Emirates Stadium, extending their lead at the top of the Premier League to nine points.

items.title: How 'irresponsible' captain Romero's week ended in 'perfect storm'
items.summary: Thomas Frank admits Tottenham captain Christian Romero's week ended in a "perfect storm" with a red card at Manchester United.

items.title: In or out? Dunfermline's Kane goes agonisingly close to opener
items.summary: Dunfermline's Chris Kane thought he had scored in his side's Scottish Cup fifth round tie with Kelty Hearts, but did the ball cross the line?

items.title: Man Utd beat 10-man Spurs for 4th straight win
items.summary: Manchester United's revival under Michael Carrick rolled on against Tottenham with goals from Bryan Mbeumo and Bruno Fernandes piling more misery on Thomas Frank's Spurs.

items.title: Viral Utd fan hopeful of haircut: It's 'going soon'!
items.summary: Viral Manchester United supporter Frank Illet his long hair is "going soon" -- over a year after he started a challenge not to have it cut until his side won five matches in a row.

items.title: Palmer breaks Chelsea's all-time hat trick record
items.summary: Cole Palmer moved clear of some Chelsea legends on Saturday as he scored his fourth hat trick for the team, setting a new club record.

items.title: Frank: No plan to strip Romero of Spurs captaincy
items.summary: Thomas Frank said that Tottenham captain Cristian Romero apologised to his teammates after being sent off in the team's defeat to Manchester United.

items.title: Celtic 'perfect fit' as Oxlade-Chamberlain signs
items.summary: Alex Oxlade-Chamberlain says Celtic is the "perfect fit" for him after joining the Scottish champions on a deal to the summer.

items.title: Tutti Quanti has Nicholls dreaming big after Newbury demolition job
items.summary: Paul Nicholls is contemplating supplementing Tutti Quanti for the Champion Hurdle after an astounding performance in the William Hill Hurdle at Newbury.

items.title: Romero cost Tottenham, not the manager - Sutton
items.summary: Jason Mohammad, Chris Sutton and Nedum Onuoha discuss Tottenham captain Cristian Romero's red card against Manchester United at Old Trafford and the impact it had on the game.
